behavior: Ground forager that runs or pauses upright while searching for invertebrates; often flicks its tail after landing. Feeds on fruit as well as animal prey, and may gather in flocks around berry-bearing trees outside the breeding season.
field_marks: Large, round-bodied thrush with long legs and a fairly long tail
Warm rusty-orange breast and belly, usually contrasting with darker upperparts
Dark gray to blackish head; females and immatures are generally paler
Yellow bill, often with a darker tip
Pale eye arcs or broken white eye ring; white lower belly and undertail coverts can show clearly
habitat: Open woodlands and forest edges, shrublands, fields and pastures, and human-altered green spaces such as gardens and parks; uses trees or shrubs for nesting and fruiting vegetation for seasonal food.
